title: End behavior of algebraic models
description: Given the graph that models a real world context, answer a question about the end behavior of the graph.

assessment_items.question_content:
A barista poured a cup of coffee.  The initial temperature of the coffee was $90^\circ\text{C}$.  As time, $t$, increased, the temperature, $C$, of the coffee began to decrease exponentially and approach room temperature of $20^\circ\text{C}$.  


**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent exponential functions

From the description, we know that the temperature of the coffee *decreased exponentially*.  Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $A$ or $D$, since $B$ and $C$ do not depict exponential decay.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that the temperature of the coffee *approached a room temperature of $\,20^\circ\text{C}$ as $t$ increased*.  This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $C=20$.  

This corresponds to graph $A$.


###The answer

The correct graph is $A$.



[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ninety) passing through the point around (five, fifty) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at C equals twenty. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2e5cf3a278ff4ddc798c734ac796a2b608c97e0)

![A linear function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ninety) and constantly decreases until reaching (seventy, zero).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fd03579d729c0df742828af7bb9ba2aca573fc49)

![A function decreases constantly as x increases on an interval from zero to fifty and becomes constant from the interval fifty to infinity on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ninety) and constantly decreases until reaching (fifty, twenty).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1b5c51d9746d1e35f77782d887b82d35a88f9bdf)

![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ninety) passing through the point around (ten, forty) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at C equals thirty. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b9e2af33e8229059a2132943f8d9e9848e41854c)

assessment_items.question_content:
In the year $2000$, there were $8{,}500$ people in the town of Wellehedge.  As the time, $t$, in years, elapsed, the population, $P$, in thousands, began to decrease exponentially and approached a population of $5{,}000$ people.  


**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent exponential functions

From the description, we know that the population of the town *decreased exponentially*.  Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $A$ or $B$, since $C$ and $D$ do not depict exponential decay.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that the population *approached $5{,}000$ people as $t$ increased*.  This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $P=5$.  (Remember, the population is in thousands.)

This corresponds to graph $B$.


###The answer

The correct graph is $B$.


[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by one and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, eight and one half) passing through the points around (five, five) and (ten, three). The asymptote is at P equals zero.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c3cd7470081cdd04f8dba4441c4a7e3aff992b08)

![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by one and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, eight and one half) passing through the points around (five, seven) and (ten, six). The asymptote is at P equals five. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a30d080b97a8560b37ecb170fad0d5d3462049e)

![A linear function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by one and is labeled t. The y axis scales by one and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, eight and one half) passing through the points around (two, five) and (five, zero). ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eb8d2cd3a5493a4e1f382c1a31c613697f60e1a3)

![A linear function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by one and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, eight and one half) passing through the points around (twenty five, seven) and (sixty, five). ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/10ff932cf93c25af0b77fc9308a635e9381fea23)

assessment_items.question_content:
The amount of carbon-$14$, $C$, in grams, remaining in a substance is *halved* every $5700$ years.  A substance initially had $20$ grams of carbon-$14$ present.  


**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship, if $t$ represents elapsed time, in thousands of years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent exponential decay

From the description, we know that the amount of carbon-$14$ in the substance *decreased* as time increased.  (Specifically, it halved every $5700$ years!) Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $C$ or $D$, since $A$ and $B$ represent increasing functions.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that the amount of carbon-$14$ in the sample is *halved* every $5700$ years.  So, as time increases, the amount of carbon-$14$ in the sample will approach $0$. [[☃ explanation 1]]  

This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $C=0$.  

This corresponds to graph $C$.

[[☃ explanation 2]]


###The answer

The correct graph is $C$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, two) passing through the point around (five, ten) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at C equals twenty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a5cc1a70920ece08bcf208ee3bf0ca35ea64a24b)

![An exponential function increases as x decreases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by two and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twenty) passing through the point around (ten, six) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at C equals zero.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/082f892290e6fc42eb92cb221ad9a76f4afabbfb)

![An exponential function increases as x decreases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by two and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twenty) passing through the point around (seven, fourteen) as it gets closer to the asymptote. assessment_items.question_content:
Rafael purchased his car for $\$12{,}000$. The value of Rafael's car depreciates over time, decreasing by $10\%$ every year.  


**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship if $t$ is elapsed time in years, and $V$ is the value of Rafael's car in thousands of dollars?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent exponential functions

From the description, we know that the value of Rafael's car *decreases by $10\%$ each year*.  This indicates exponential decay.  

Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $C$ or $D$, since $A$ and $B$ do not depict exponential decay.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

Since the value of Rafael's car decreases by $10\%$ each year, as time increases, the value of Rafael's car will approach $0$. [[☃ explanation 1]]  

This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $V=0$.  


This corresponds to graph $C$.

[[☃ explanation 2]]
###The answer

The correct graph is $C$.

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Initially, $5$ students at Noa's school came down with the flu.  As time, $t$, increased, the number of people, $N$, who came down with the flu increased rapidly, and then slowly approached $50$.  

**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent increasing functions

From the description, we know that the number of people who came down with the flu *increased* as time increased.  Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $A$ or $D$, since $B$ and $C$ represent decreasing functions.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that  as time increased, the number of people who came down with the flu increased rapidly and then *slowly approached $50$*.  This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $N=50$.  

This corresponds to graph $A$.

###The answer

The correct graph is $A$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, five) increasing through the point (two, thirty) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at N equals fifty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5af1c3a2dad2bf2c2d1fe7726b18edd4534bbfd0)

The asymptote is at N equals five.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36e7868f1bb2940b8c800fb12d2443dab969ef6a)

![A linear function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by four and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled N. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fifty) decreasing through the point (twenty, five).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3761b5ba0fbafa08625a3f841330203d6f41f82a)

assessment_items.question_content:
Before its grand opening, Crazy Cupcakes spent $\$2{,}000$ on advertising.    As time, $t$, increased, the total amount of money, $A$, in thousands of dollars, that Crazy Cupcakes spent on advertising increased rapidly, and then slowly approached $\$10{,}000$.  

**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent increasing functions

From the description, we know that the total amount of money that Crazy Cupcakes spent on advertising  *increased* as time increased.  Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $A$ or $B$, since $C$ and $D$ represent decreasing functions.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that as time increased, the total amount of money that Crazy Cupcakes spent on advertising increased rapidly, and then *slowly approached $\$10{,}000$*.   This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $A=10$.  

This corresponds to graph $B$.

###The answer

The correct graph is $B$.


[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
The atmospheric pressure at sea level is $14.7$ pounds per square inch $(\text{lb}/{\text{in.}}^2)$.  For each increase in a distance of $3.6$ miles above sea level, the atmospheric pressure is halved. 

**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship if $d$ is the distance above sea level, in miles, and $P$ is the atmospheric pressure, in $\text{lb}/{\text{in.}}^2$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent decreasing functions

From the description, we know that the atmospheric pressure  *decreases* as distance from sea level increases.  (Specifically, it is cut in half every $3.6$ miles.) Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $B$ or $C$, since $A$ and $D$ represent increasing functions.

###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that the atmospheric pressure is *halved* every $3.6$ miles.  So, as distance increases, the atmospheric pressure will approach $0$. [[☃ explanation 1]]  

This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $P=0$.  

This corresponds to graph $C$.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

###The answer

The correct graph is $C$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fourteen and seven tenths) increasing as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals twenty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d96a06459191670536bb3d029c5f69be92fc21b1)

![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by four and is labeled d. The y axis scales by four and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fourteen and seven tenths) decreasing as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals three and six tenths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/937005c3b1e7f27b17b4371298681e10670bc5aa)

![An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by four and is labeled d. The y axis scales by four and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fourteen and seven tenths) passing through the point around (two, ten) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals zero.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2c387c9323b799fc3850674edd2108ef4c01c97)

![A linear function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by five and is labeled d. The y axis scales by five and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fourteen and seven tenths) and constantly increases passing through the point around (ten, twenty) going towards infinity.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a167d4b19e6a871a6a5dbe13c43f2e43b4b2cb6)

assessment_items.question_content:
Initially, there were $20$ wolves living within a nature preserve.  As time, $t$, increased, the population of wolves, $P$, increased rapidly at first, and then slowly began to approach $50$.  

**Which of the following graphs could model this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Choosing the graphs that represent increasing functions

From the description, we know that the number of wolves living within the nature preserve *increased* as time increased.  Therefore, we know that the correct answer must be $B$, $C$, or $D$, since $A$ represents a decreasing function.


###Choosing the graph with the correct end behavior

We are also told that as time increased, the wolf population increased rapidly, and then *slowly approached $50$*. This means that the graph of the function has a *horizontal asymptote* at $P=50$.  

This corresponds to graph $D$.


###The answer

The correct graph is $D$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, fifty) passing through the point around (ten, twenty five) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals twenty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fbe1fb7fe171449302cd03203a2f0e5fef9c608e)

![An exponential function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twenty) passing through the point around (five, thirty five) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals forty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90d43b6808d43ef4f7b2dfdd468f14a8eda946f2)

![A linear function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twenty) passing through the point around (ten, twenty five) and constantly increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/677bb4689e5ac156c1fa5ed5a676223993a454d8)

![An exponential function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twenty) passing through the point around (ten, forty five) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals fifty.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af7d3c7262505c148196341e7a874f77cedfce48)

assessment_items.question_content:
After the closing of the mills, the population of the town starts decreasing exponentially. 

The graph below represents the population, $P$, in thousands, of the town $t$ years after the closing of the mill.


[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, with the mill closed, what does the population of the town approach as time increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the value that the population approaches as time increases.  

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $P$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $P=20$.


[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

The population of the town is approaching $20{,}000$.

options.alt: An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by two and is labeled t. The y axis scales by five and is labeled P. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, forty) passing through the points around (four, thirty) and (eight, twenty five). The asymptote is at P equals twenty.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tanya purchased a cup of coffee at the coffee shop. 

The graph below represents the temperature of the coffee, $C$, in degrees Celsius, $t$ minutes after the coffee was served.



[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, what temperature does the coffee approach as time increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the value that the temperature of the coffee approaches as time increases.  

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $C$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $C=20$.


[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

The coffee is approaching a temperature of $20^\circ\text{C}$.

options.alt: An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by five and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled C. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ninety) passing through the point around (fifteen, twenty five) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at C equals twenty.

choices.content: $20^{\circ}\text{C}$
$30^{\circ}\text{C}$
$60^{\circ}\text{C}$
$90^{\circ}\text{C}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There is an outbreak of the flu at Burak's school. 

The graph below represents the number of people at Burak's school, $N$, who have been infected with the flu $t$ days after the initial outbreak.  

[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, about how many people were infected with the flu during this outbreak?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the number of people at Burak's school that have been infected with the flu during this outbreak.  

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $N$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $N=40$.


[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

About $40$ people were infected with the flu during this outbreak.

options.alt: An exponential function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by two and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled N. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, five) passing through the point around (two, twenty five) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at N equals forty.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jackson posts a funny video on his blog. 

The graph below represents the total views, $V$, that his video receives after being posted on his blog for $t$ days.

[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, about how many views will Jackson's video receive in total?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the approximate number of views Jackson's video will receive in total.  

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $V$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $V=200$.

[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

Jackson's video will receive about $200$ views.

options.alt: An exponential function increases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by fifty and is labeled V. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, ten) passing through the point around (five, one hundred seventy) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at V equals two hundred.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elena knows that Lea has a garden with an area of $100$ square meters.  She does not know the specific width, $w$, and length, $l$ of the garden.

The graph below represents the widths that correspond to  possible lengths of Lea's garden. 

[[☃ image 1]]



**As the possible width of Lea's garden increases, what does its  possible length approach?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the value that the possible length of the garden approaches as the possible width of the garden increases. 

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $w$ approaches infinity, what does $l$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $l=0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

As the width of Lea's garden increases, the length of it approaches $0$.

options.alt: A hyperbolic function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled w. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled l. The graph starts at the a vertical asymptote at the y axis at from infinity passing through the point around (five, twenty) as it gets closer to the horizontal asymptote. The horizontal asymptote is at l equals zero.

choices.content: $\infty$

assessment_items.question_content:
Isabella is researching how the population of her hometown has changed over time and comes up with a model to predict its future growth.

The graph below represents the population, $P$, in thousands, of Isabella's hometown, where $t$ represents the number of years since $1900$.  


[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, what will the population of the town approach as time increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the value that the population will approach as time increases.  

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $P$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $P=50$.


[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

The population of the town will approach $50{,}000$.

options.alt: A function increases at varying rates as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by twenty and is labeled t. The y axis scales by ten and is labeled P. The graph's lesser horizontal asymptote is near P equals 0. The graph starts near the y axis around (zero, zero) passing through the points around (forty, five) and (seventy, thirty five) as it gets closer to the greater horizontal asymptote. The asymptote is at P equals fifty.

assessment_items.question_content:
A committee is tasked with cutting about $10\%$ of their company's annual excess spending each day until the company's annual budget is balanced. 

The graph below represents the company's total annual spending, $S$, in thousands of dollars, approved by the committee after $t$ days of meetings.


[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, what is the company's budget (assuming it is balanced)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the company's balanced budget, which will be met as time increases. 

Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $S$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.   [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $S=8$.



[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

The company's annual budget is $\$8{,}000$.

options.alt: An exponential function decreases as x increases on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by ten and is labeled t. The y axis scales by two and is labeled S. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, twelve) passing through the point around (twelve, nine) as it gets closer to the asymptote. The asymptote is at S equals eight.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rumor is started by two students at Belldale High School.  

The graph below represents the number of students, $N$, who have heard the rumor $t$ hours after it was started.   



[[☃ image 1]]



**Based on the graph, about how many students will hear the rumor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

We want to find the number of students who hear the rumor.  


Mathematically, this corresponds to the following question.

$\qquad$ As $t$ approaches infinity, what does $N$ approach?  

This question reflects the definition of a horizontal asymptote, and so to answer the question, we need only find the horizontal asymptote of the graph.  [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the horizontal asymptote

To find the horizontal asymptote of this function, we can draw a horizontal line that models what the function approaches as time increases.

In this case, we see that this line has the equation $N=160$.


[[☃ image 1]]

###The answer

About $160$ students will hear the rumor.

options.alt: A graph on an x y coordinate plane. The x axis scales by five and is labeled t. The y axis scales by twenty and is labeled N. The graph starts at the y axis at (zero, zero) and increases as x increases. It goes closer to the asymptote at y equals one hundred sixty.
